dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic had the strongest direct HN discussion: Claude was criticized for greenfield design limits, but commenters still described it as fast, useful delegated coding help and saw Cowork as a promising workflow product [E1][E2]. Secret-handling worries around Claude Code kept the day from being a clean sweep, yet Anthropic still had the broadest and most favorable provider-specific coverage [E3][E6].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic won because it was the only tracked provider with multiple substantive front-page stories centered on its products, and the comment sections leaned toward practical usefulness rather than dismissal. Praise for Claude's day-to-day coding value and interest in Claude Cowork outweighed one concrete security/sandboxing concern, while other providers only showed up in much thinner single-story comparison signals [E1][E2][E3][E6].

openai.judgementSnippet: A lone comparison comment said gpt-5.2 declined a suspicious link while gpt-oss models followed it, so the day only produced a thin mixed signal about safety behavior [E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude discussion centered on practical coding usefulness and interest in Cowork, with commenters often treating limits as something to manage with prompting and workflow rather than as product failure; the main drag was concern that Claude Code session data and secrets need tighter sandboxing [E1][E2][E3][E6].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini appeared in one comparison comment where gemini-3-pro was said to refuse suspicious short links, which reads as a minor safety win but not a broad verdict on the product [E5].

evidence.summary: The title framed Claude as weak at creating new blocks, but top comments mostly emphasized that it is already great at low-level dev work, multi-file edits, and delegated tasks, with some arguing the limitation is partly a minimal-edit bias.

The Claude Cowork thread called its handling of a detailed task impressive and described Cowork as a sensible productization of latent demand, even as commenters questioned whether typical users can prompt that well.

In the Bubblewrap thread, commenters warned that Claude Code's local .claude directory contains sensitive session transcripts and could be vulnerable to prompt-injection exfiltration, making secret handling a real concern.
A single comment said gpt-5.2 refused suspicious-looking short links while gpt-oss 20B and 120B eagerly visited them, leaving only a sparse mixed safety signal for OpenAI models.
One comparison comment reported that gemini-3-pro refused to traverse the suspicious short links, a small safety-positive datapoint.
That same comparison comment reported that Opus 4.5 refused the suspicious short links, adding a small safety-positive signal for Anthropic.
